cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The increasing interconnection of economies, cultures, and populations worldwide through trade, communication, and migration

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: International organization established in 1995 to promote free trade by reducing tariffs and settling trade disputes between member nations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic policy advocating free markets, privatization, deregulation, and reduced government spending, promoted by the IMF and World Bank to developing nations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: International financial institution that provides loans to developing nations, often with conditions requiring free-market reforms

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The movement of manufacturing from developed to developing countries seeking lower labor costs, accelerated by trade liberalization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The revolution in computing, internet connectivity, and digital communications that accelerated globalization from the 1990s onward

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The long-term warming of Earth's climate caused primarily by greenhouse gas emissions from burning fossil fuels, industrialization, and deforestation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: International treaty framework that aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions to limit global warming, updated through summits like COP21 (Paris 2015)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Rapid economic growth in previously developing nations like Brazil, Russia, India, and China (BRIC countries) fueled by globalization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Chinese government's economic reform program beginning in 1978 under Deng Xiaoping that opened China to foreign investment and market economics

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Deng Xiaoping
cards.answer: Chinese leader who launched economic reforms in 1978 that opened China to foreign investment, creating a 'socialist market economy' that transformed China into a global economic power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1992 trade agreement between the US, Canada, and Mexico that eliminated most tariffs and integrated North American economies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political and economic union of European nations that established a common currency (euro) and removed trade and immigration barriers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The use of violent tactics by non-state groups to achieve political goals, often targeting civilians, that became a major global security concern after 9/11

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: September 11 attacks on the United States

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The global political and military response to 9/11 launched by the United States, involving wars in Afghanistan and Iraq

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The large-scale movement of people across national borders driven by poverty, conflict, and environmental degradation in the late 20th and early 21st centuries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The spread of American cultural products (fast food, films, music) and English language globally, seen as cultural dominance or homogenization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Political and social movements resisting globalization, emphasizing national sovereignty, cultural protection, and economic protectionism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 2008 global financial crisis triggered by the collapse of the US housing market, demonstrating the interconnectedness of global financial systems

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The AIDS pandemic that spread globally from the 1980s onward, disproportionately affecting Sub-Saharan Africa and highlighting health inequalities

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Multinational corporations that operate in multiple countries, shifting production to low-wage regions and transforming global labor markets

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1994 Rwandan genocide in which Hutu extremists killed approximately 800,000 Tutsi and moderate Hutu in 100 days

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Rwandan Genocide

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The UN principle developed in the 2000s that sovereign nations have a duty to protect civilians, and if they fail, the international community can intervene

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 2001 terrorist attacks by al-Qaeda on the United States that killed nearly 3,000 people and triggered a global War on Terror

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The World Wide Web becomes publicly available, enabling the modern internet

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The process by which production is spread across multiple countries, with each country specializing in specific components based on comparative advantage

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1994-1995 genocide in Rwanda in which Hutu extremists killed approximately 800,000 Tutsi people in 100 days, raising questions about humanitarian intervention

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The spread of democratic governance and liberal economic policies in Eastern Europe and Latin America following the end of the Cold War

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Deng Xiaoping
cards.answer: Chinese leader who introduced market-oriented economic reforms after 1978 (Opening and Reform Policy), transforming China into the world's second-largest economy while maintaining Communist Party rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The framework of international institutions (UN, IMF, World Bank, WTO) created after WWII to manage global cooperation and prevent another world war

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Countries in East Asia (South Korea, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Singapore) that achieved rapid economic growth through export-led industrialization in the late 20th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The long-term trend of increasing average global temperatures due to greenhouse gas emissions from burning fossil fuels

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The worldwide financial crisis (2007–2009) triggered by the collapse of the US housing market, causing the deepest global recession since the Great Depression

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The movement of people across international borders, driven by economic opportunity, political instability, or environmental factors, reshaping demographics in receiving countries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Microfinance
cards.answer: The provision of small loans and financial services to poor people without access to traditional banking, pioneered by Muhammad Yunus and Grameen Bank in Bangladesh

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The worldwide movement demanding action on climate change, environmental protection, and sustainable development, increasingly influential in global politics since the 1990s

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Kyoto Protocol on climate change is adopted, requiring industrialized nations to reduce greenhouse gas emissions

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The principle that all nations have the right to self-determination and that the international community has a responsibility to protect civilians from genocide and war crimes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: International organizations like the UN, World Bank, and WHO that operate beyond the authority of any single nation-state in managing global issues

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The spread of American cultural products (music, film, fast food, fashion) around the world, seen by some as cultural imperialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political and religious movement seeking to return to fundamentalist interpretations of religion as a response to secularization and cultural globalization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: India's economic liberalization begins, integrating India into the global economy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The AIDS/HIV epidemic that spread globally from the 1980s onwards, killing over 36 million people and disproportionately affecting Sub-Saharan Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The process by which governments reduce their role in the economy by selling state-owned enterprises to private investors

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political movement demanding women's equality in economic, social, and political life, gaining momentum globally in the 20th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mikhail Gorbachev
cards.answer: Last leader of the Soviet Union who introduced glasnost (openness) and perestroika (restructuring) to modernize the USSR; his reforms inadvertently led to the Soviet Union's collapse in 1991

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: NAFTA goes into effect, eliminating most tariffs between the US, Canada, and Mexico

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The process of increasing worldwide interconnectedness through the movement of goods, capital, information, and people across national borders
